entry.title: Can't find the role「SAP_SDF_ALM_METRIC_PUSH_HEALTH」in S/4HANA with ST-PI 740 SP18
entry.content:
We need to monitor the RISE S/4HANA system with SAP Cloud ALM.
The system has ST-PI 740 SP18 installed, but the role 「SAP_SDF_ALM_METRIC_PUSH_HEALTH」does not exist on the system.
How to get the roles for SAP Cloud ALM Operation monitoring?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Can't find the role「SAP_SDF_ALM_METRIC_PUSH_HEALTH」in...
entry.content:
• The Roles for metric push to SAP Cloud ALM can be found and downloaded from the note:
    3372078 - Update of ST-PI 7.40 delivered PFCG roles, relevant for the metric push to SAP Cloud ALM

  • As technical prerequisites for the monitoring of the ABAP systems, it is recommended to update the ST-PI to the latest the latest or second latest Support Package.
    Please review the expert portal guide: SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite 7

entry.content:

If it exists, I would like to know its URL.

entry.title: Different VAT number when PO raised to vendor in other country
entry.content:
I have company code in UK with one VAT number and the same company code have different VAT number in Austria. When I raise PO to vendor in Austria, I want Austrian VAT number of company code to be displayed in the print in place of source country VAT number. How can I get this achieved.

Regards

Venkatesh

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification for Partners: An Overview of the Latest Changes - Including Q&A
entry.content:
Thank you @Vitaly_Toraman for the suggestions. Unfortunately I am not seeing the option "access SAP learning rooms". On screenshare, last weekend, the SAP Helpdesk Support was trying to guide through same but couldn't proceed beyond. 

Hi cdabrowski, Thank you attending to my comment. I already have Case with them (Case ID : 5000123926). I was told "In checking your account I do not see the access available to you. For this you will need to reach out to the SAP Store team directly via the link below: (SAP store link was provide). If you wish to subscribe to SAP Certification Hub 1 or 6 exam attempts see here under Become certified".

That didn't help. From the quoted excerpt above and my interaction so far, it appears to me that the 2 attempts subscription is relatively new/ non-regular and hence not that recognized within the Helpdesks even. I can only guess though. Most likely I will be buying the 6 attempts subscription now. But as of now, issue remains far from resolved.

Thank you.

entry.title: ABAP Cloud Project in Eclipse
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We have a BTP account and have several sub accounts in it which do not have ABAP instance setup in them.

Can we still add these sub accounts to the ABAP eclipse or we need to create an ABAP instance for them first ?

We would like to debug our app when we run it in these sub accounts by setting them up in the eclipse.

Appreciate your inputs.

entry.content:
Best Web Design Company in Delhi – Professional Website Development Services by Dadhich Technologies

 

 

Are you looking for the best web design company in Delhi to create a stunning, fast, and SEO-friendly website for your business? Look no further!

 

In today’s digital world, having a professional website is not a luxury — it’s a necessity. Whether you are a startup, small business, or a large company, a well-designed website helps you build your online presence, generate leads, and grow your business.

 

Dadhich Technologies is the best web development company in Delhi, providing affordable and customized website design and development services for all types of businesses.

 

 

 

 

Why Choose Dadhich Technologies as Your Web Design Partner?

 

 

We are a trusted web design company in Delhi known for delivering high-quality websites that drive results. Our experienced team of website developers, designers, and digital marketing experts work together to bring your vision to life.

 

 

What Makes Us The Best Website Development Company in Delhi?

 

 

  • Customized Website Design

Our Web Design & Development Services in Delhi

We create modern and creative website designs that reflect your brand identity. Our design approach is user-friendly, clean, and conversion-focused.

5. Shopify Developer in Delhi

 

 

Start your online store with the help of the best Shopify developers in Delhi. From theme customization to payment setup — we handle it all.

 

 

 

 

Benefits of Hiring a Professional Web Design Company in Delhi

  2. Get a Mobile-Friendly Website

Who Can Hire Us?

Why Businesses Trust Dadhich Technologies?

We have already delivered successful projects for multiple clients across India and globally. Our focus is always on customer satisfaction, timely delivery, and building long-term relationships.

entry.title: 批量打印生产订单
entry.content:
求助SAP专家：

是否可以实现根据某一条件进行生产工单的筛选，例如某个时间段，或者某些单号。从众多的生产工单中选择出来，然后一次性批量打印？
目前APP Production Order Display ，只能查看一个生产订单去打印；或者APP Manage production orders 可以展示很多生产工单，但也不能做到挑选出需要的工单然后一次性全部打印。
是否有APP或者替代方案可以做到类似于Account document output 这个APP可以批量去打印财务凭证一样去批量打印生产工单？

或者是需要如何设置呢  谢谢

entry.title: FB60 Vendor posting for witholding tax
entry.content:
Hi All.

I have query on when in FB60 Vendor posting for witholding tax.

The amout is 10k, 8% of withholding tax at 800.

My current SAP settings is

Credit Vendor account 9200
Debit COGS account 10k
Credit Tax payable GL 800

Now user expectation is

Expecting entry -

Cr Vendor 10k
Cr Tax payable 800
DR GR/IR 10k
DR Vendor 800

Can please advise on the system setting/configuration?

Thanks

Q&A for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - How to ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Drop down field Manage Configuration UI
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Thank you for reaching out. This is Hannah from Employee Central Team. Regarding your query - How to create drop down field with answers no/yes in the Manage Configuration UI:

It is possible to create drop down field with picklist no/yes by following steps:

1. go to "Configure object definition" >> find the object, add custom field, make sure that:

data type = picklist, Valid Values Source = Picklist id (which is configured in Picklist Center)

and save.

2. go to "Manage configuration UI" >> find the UI, add field, make sure that:

value type = resolver, Value Type Resolver = custom field （which is configured in step 1）

and save.

 

Please refer to Screenshot 1.png

 

Best regards,

Hannah

entry.title: Re: Drop down field Manage Configuration UI
entry.content: Here is the screenshot

entry.title: Re: 交易幣別與功能幣別的匯率只能預設抓取IMG的設定，是否有方法讓USER自行調整匯率?

entry.title: Introducing the New PaPM Step in Multi Actions in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Integrating SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) with SAP Profitability and Performance Management (PaPM) offers significant business value by combining powerful data visualization and planning capabilities with advanced profitability and allocation calculations. 

SAC users can execute PaPM calculations via API step within Multi actions today. API step in Multi Actions empowers SAC users to execute remote calculation or program as one step in a multi action sequence. It requires technical background to properly configure the API step.

I am excited to introduce the upcoming release of integrating PaPM step within multi actions, which will be available with our 2025 QRC2 release!  This new capability offers easy-to-use configurations, which empowers a non-IT user to create the streamlined workflows and improve efficiency.

What’s more, in the 2025 QRC2 release, SAC is introducing enhanced parameterizations for String type parameter and Datetime type parameter.

 

The PaPM Step in Multi Actions

A new dedicated step type in multi actions for PaPM integration

The PaPM integration step is now in a more prominent place in multi actions, making it more accessible for users.

 

 

Easy-to-use PaPM step configuration

Adding a PaPM step to your multi action is a straightforward process. As a user, your knowledge about the PaPM process is sufficient to easily configure the PaPM step in multi actions. You don’t need to bother about the complexity of the technical details of the HTTP APIs.

Here are the key steps involved:

  1. Enter a Name: Provide a name for the step to easily identify it within your multi action.
  2. Choose PaPM Connection: Select the appropriate PaPM connection to link your SAC tenant with PaPM system.  Note that the HTTP API connection created for the API step is not applicable in this context.
  3. Select Action: Decide whether you want to use an existing process or create a new one.
  4. Configure Activity: Depending on your choice of action, select the relevant PaPM environment, process, template, and activity.
  5. Set Parameters: Define the parameters for the PaPM step. You can choose pre-defined multi actions parameters or set fixed values.
  6. Choose Selections: Specify the selections for the PaPM step to tailor it to your needs. You can choose pre-defined multi actions parameters or set fixed values.
  7. Follow-Up Options: Decide if you want to delete the PaPM process after it runs or set the activity to complete if successful.

Once the PaPM integration step is added to the multi action, you can continue to add more steps, and reorder steps as required. This flexibility allows you to create comprehensive workflows that leverage the full capabilities of SAP Analytics Cloud and PaPM.

 

Enhanced parameterization strengthens the integration with PaPM

The current Member type parameter and Number type parameter are applicable to the new PaPM step. These parameters can be used across all existing steps and the new PaPM step.  

Additionally, the QRC2 2025 release introduces a new String type parameter and a new Datetime type parameter. These new parameters can be used in the PaPM step and the API step.

The String type parameter offers various options to easily choose the right values during multi actions execution.

  • The available values of the String type parameter can be bound with a PaPM parameter ensuring only the values allowed by the PaPM parameter can be chosen during multi actions execution.

  • The available values of the String type parameter can be predefined in the multi actions (for example, yes or no, true or false).

  • The available values of the String type parameter can also be a free string, allowing the full flexibility of any string during multi actions execution.

 

The Datetime type parameter is also introduced, providing a date picker and datetime picker for value selection. The date and datetime picker ensure accurate and efficient input, improving user experience and interaction with the product.

 

 

Execute Multi Action
This multi action can be executed in story, analytics application, and scheduled via Calendar as normal multi action.

Now, planner can execute this multi action via one button / schedule job simply. The complexity of planning process is hidden from end-user.

 

Example Use Case

Imagine a scenario where a company wants to analyze its profitability across different product lines and regions. By integrating PaPM step within a multi action in SAP Analytics Cloud, the company can automate the process of running profitability calculations in PaPM, gathering the calculation from PaPM to SAC and running further calculations in SAC.

Here's how the multi action works:

  1. Profitability Calculation: The PaPM step is triggered to run profitability calculations based on the collected data. This step leverages the Data Export Service and PaPM's advanced algorithms to pull data from SAC to PaPM and perform detailed calculations.
  2. Data Collection: Once the calculations are complete, the multi action proceeds to import the calculated profitability data via Data Import step.
  3. Data aggregation and consolidation: the profitability across different product lines and regions are aggregated and consolidated via the Data Action step.

By automating these steps within a multi action, the company can save time, reduce errors, and ensure that profitability analysis is conducted efficiently and effectively.

 

Conclusion

The integration of PaPM steps within multi actions in SAP Analytics Cloud marks a significant advancement in simplifying and streamlining the workflow. This new feature not only enhances the efficiency and accuracy of workflows but also provides users with the flexibility to tailor processes to their specific needs.

As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the modern market, tools like these will be invaluable in driving success and growth

entry.title: Re: Exchange Rate Difference Line with Zero Amount Preven...
entry.content: Dear Jeremy, Thank you for your reply. We find that a group currency amount difference of 0.01 USD is generated between the down payment and accounts payable. However, all of our current transactions are conducted in TWD. Whether this exchange difference is intentionally retained by the system when the invoice is cleared with a down payment, or is it caused by exchange rate rounding differences due to multiple line items being calculated separately instead of based on the total amount (i.e., could this issue occur in scenarios beyond just down payments)? I've uploaded the screenshots in the article, thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS1: TM master data based Sales Order Scheduling
entry.content:
Hi @Michael_Haase ,

Thanks a lot for this blog. Is it possible to get distance and duration from GIS provider (HSS/PC Miler)? I see API call is one if option for transit time, but do we need to write separate logic to call GIS service or existing standard HSS call for DDD can be leveraged? We want to get the transit time from GIS since business will not be using Default routes and lanes will be very generic (Zone to Zone). Kindly share your thoughts.

Regards,

Karthik

entry.title: Re: Unable to subscribe to SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: Does anyone know a solution to this issue?

entry.title: How to call BuildProcessAutomation's TriggerAPI from RAP
entry.content:
I'm trying to call the BuildProcessAutomation TriggerAPI from RAP, but it's not working.
Could you please tell me how to solve this problem or give me some sample code?

The call was successful when using the Development in Classic ABAP method, but when I coded in the determination on Save section, an error occurred at runtime.
I would like to use the Development in ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) method.

See below

Development in ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP) | SAP Help Portal

 

Test Code(on determination on Save section)

CONSTANTS: wf_definitionId   TYPE if_swf_cpwf_api=>cpwf_def_id_long  VALUE 'us10.bdg1-customer-g1vd58ff.testonefield.test_one_field',
           wf_retention_days TYPE if_swf_cpwf_api=>retention_time VALUE '30',
           consumer          TYPE string VALUE 'DEFAULT'.

**** Define the structure of the payload
 types:begin of context,
   field1 type string,
 end of context.

  TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_context,
    context TYPE context,
  END OF ty_context.

 TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_payload,
   start_event TYPE ty_context,
 END OF ty_payload  .

TRY.
* Get the API of workflow
  DATA Wf_handle     TYPE sysuuid_c32.
  DATA(lo_cpwf_api) = cl_swf_cpwf_api_factory_a4c=>get_api_instance( ).

**convert workflow data to be passed in json format
  data lv_context type ty_context.
  lv_context-context-field1 = 'test_value'.

  DATA(context_json) = lo_cpwf_api->get_start_context_from_data(
        iv_capital_letter  = abap_false
*        it_name_mapping    = VALUE #( ( abap = 'CONTEXT' json = 'CONTEXT' ) )
        iv_data = lv_context
        ).


*trigger workflow
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_cpwf_inst
      ENTITY CPWFInstance
      EXECUTE registerWorkflow
      FROM VALUE #( (
*                      %key = '<a UUID which is mandatory for mass enabled callers>'
                      %param-RetentionTime = 30
                      %param-PaWfDefId = wf_definitionId
*                      %param-CpWfDefId = ''
*                      %param-CallbackClass = '<Callback class for final state handling>'
                      %param-Consumer = 'DEFAULT'
                    ) )
     MAPPED DATA(mapped_wf)
     FAILED DATA(failed_wf)
     REPORTED DATA(reported_wf).

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_cpwf_inst
      ENTITY CPWFInstance
      EXECUTE setPayload
      FROM VALUE #( (
*                      %key-CpWfHandle = '<required key (UUID) of the CPWF instance>'
                      %key-CpWfHandle = mapped_wf-cpwfinstance[ 1 ]-CpWfHandle
                      %param-context =  context_json ) )
      MAPPED mapped_wf
      FAILED failed_wf
      REPORTED reported_wf.

Dear team,

9686669350

entry.title: Unexpected Credit Check Error During PGI Without an assigned Credit Segment
entry.content:
Hello Experts , 

We are experiencing an issue with the credit management setup in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. If the Credit Segment is not maintained for the respective Sales Area, why does the system still throw a credit-related error during Post Goods Issue (PGI)?


According to the standard O2C/OTC process behavior, the absence of an assigned Credit Segment for a specific Sales Area typically indicates that credit management should not be active for that combination.

As a result, the system should not perform a credit check, and no credit-related error should occur.
However, in our current scenario, the system unexpectedly triggers a credit error during PGI, even though no Credit Segment is maintained for the Sales Area in question.

This behavior seems contrary to standard functionality, and we are trying to understand why the system enforces a credit check under these circumstances.


Is this an expected behavior under certain configurations or conditions, or could this indicate a misconfiguration? Any insights or suggestions for troubleshooting would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Data Migration Cockpit Date issue for all migration o...
entry.content:
Whenever we are facing issue with Valid from date and valid to date, for uploading the file with Migrate your data. please follow the below steps.

Valid From Date: 01/01/2025 (DD/MM/YYYY) Enter in this format 

Valid To Date: 12/31/2025(MM/DD/YYYY) Enter in this format

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP managed scenario dumping CX_CSP_ACT_INTERNAL

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @DavidRaykovitz ,

Sorry for the later reply, not sure you still face the same situation as described. If I understand correct, that in production the employee name is different between LMS and BizX, but in staging the employee name is the same. Have you tried to disable "Enable adoption of General Display Name" and re-enable it again in Production? That will re-trigger the data sync between LMS and BizX, if the issue is not fixed, would you please report an issue to LMS, to further check it within SF.

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Adding PARAMETERS style functionality to SAP ABAP BTP
entry.content:
In traditional SAPGUI based ABAP reports, we use PARAMETERS to collect input from the user, and WRITE statements to provide some output.

With web based front ends, this is no longer possible, which led me to a possible solution.

We have two new tables, which hold parameter name/value pairs for each user, and outputs. The parameters are filled by presenting a simple HTML form to the user, where they can update any values. They can also provide the name of a global class, and a method name. These are what the developer builds from a global class, and static methods. The names of the methods are not important, but let's assume there is an INIT, which sets some parameters, and a MAIN which processes the inputs.

The INIT method might look like this:

zmn_getsetparams=>hideparams(  ).

  zmn_getsetparams=>setparam( description = 'Integer1' parname = 'INT1' parvalue = '66' sequence = '01' overwrite = abap_false ).
  zmn_getsetparams=>setparam( description = 'Integer2' parname = 'INT2' parvalue = '33' sequence = '03' overwrite = abap_false ).
  zmn_getsetparams=>setparam( description = 'Math operator' parname = 'OPERATOR' parvalue = '+' sequence = '02' overwrite = abap_false ).
  zmn_getsetparams=>clearoutput(  ).
  zmn_getsetparams=>write( |Initialized parameters| ).

The hideparams() method sets a flag for all existing parameters, so that the user doesn't se them on the HTML form.

The clearouput() method clears the output, and sets a simple status message.

An example of the MAIN method might look like this:

 DATA(pa_int1) = CONV i(  zmn_getsetparams=>getparam( 'INT1' ) ).
    DATA(pa_int2) = CONV i(  zmn_getsetparams=>getparam( 'INT2' ) ).

    DATA result TYPE string.

    CASE zmn_getsetparams=>getparam( 'OPERATOR' ).

      WHEN '+'.
        result = |{  pa_int1 }+{ pa_int2 } = { pa_int1 + pa_int2 }|.
      WHEN '-'.
        result = |{  pa_int1 }-{ pa_int2 } = { pa_int1 - pa_int2 }|.
      WHEN '*'.
        result = |{  pa_int1 }*{ pa_int2 } = { pa_int1 * pa_int2 }|.
      WHEN '/'.
        IF pa_int2 IS INITIAL.
          result = 'No division by zero allowed'.
        ELSE.
          result = |{  pa_int1 }/{ pa_int2 } = { conv f( pa_int1 / pa_int2 ) DECIMALS  = 2 }|.
        ENDIF.
      WHEN OTHERS.
        result = |Bad operator: {  zmn_getsetparams=>getparam( 'OPERATOR' ) }|.
    ENDCASE.
zmn_getsetparams=>clearoutput(  ).
zmn_getsetparams=>write( |=================Demo 2===========| ).
zmn_getsetparams=>write( result ).

This returns the values as entered by the user, and prepares a simple result of the calculation.

 

The code can be found under https://github.com/michaelnicholls/ZPARAMS.git, with some setup instructions for both on-premise and BTP ABAP environments.

entry.title: Re: Conditionally send a standard event from SAP S/4HANA...
entry.content: Thanks @JChristophNolte for your inputs. I understand the event-driven architecture should not be loaded with too much data. My question was rather in the context of event producer's side, in this case, SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, if there is any possibility to suppress event publishing conditionally and locally inside S/4HANA. I would also have loved change documents in SAP S/4HANA exposed as API in this case.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate run of integration flow consuming event from event mesh of SAP Cloud Integration Suite
entry.content: Hi @ajmaradiaga, Would you please take a look at this?

entry.title: How can I upload mass vendor invoices, any standard FIORI application or Tcode ?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, need your help please!

How can I upload mass vendor invoices, any standard FIORI application or Tcode please ?
I tried "Upload General Journal Entries" but it doesn't work for vendor invoices, if someone can confirm this please ?

I'm wondering if there an other similar FIORI application for vendor invoices or if there is any workaround solution ?

Thank you in advance,

MEDKHA

entry.title: Re: General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024 Universal First Half 2025
entry.content:
Hi @TracyH ,

If I understand correct, you change the label of firstName from ‘First Name’ to ‘Preferred Name’, and the label of preferredName from 'Preferred Name' to 'Legal First Name', and configured name format for General Display Name as firstName + lastName. For employee name display, I don't have concern with that.

But such semantic meaning change can affect some features, such as, in some cases if legal name need to be displayed instead, eg. hiring new employee/doc sign/comp form, system will retrieve employee name from firstName and lastName, then Preferred Name + Last Name will be retrieved and displayed instead. And, in integration scenario, like payroll integration, firstName and lastName are synced as legal name and used in payroll, then actually Preferred Name will be synced. Please take that into consideration.

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: In my BTP trial account trust configuration option missing
entry.content: same issue her

entry.title: Re: In my BTP trial account trust configuration option mi...

entry.title: CDS view for Master recipe in public cloud 2502

Hi 

Need a released CDS view in public cloud 2502 for master recipe in which activity quantity & work center fields are provided. Please suggest a combine CDS view.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Events: Initial Loads using RAP Events
entry.content:
Hi @TimoMaier,

I understand the technical approach outlined in this blog post.  However, could you please clarify what you mean by "initial loads" in this context?  It would also be helpful if you could share a real-world business use case where this approach would be applicable.

Thanks,
Mustafa.

entry.title: Need API to Fetch Customer Hierarchies in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and I need to programmatically retrieve Customer Hierarchies (e.g., parent-child relationships between sold-to, payer, bill-to, ship-to partners, etc.). After reviewing the available APIs in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, I have not found an OData or SOAP API that explicitly supports fetching customer hierarchies or customer master relationship data.

I have already explored the following:

  • Business Partner APIs (API_BUSINESS_PARTNER)

  • Available CDS Views and Communication Scenarios

None of these seem to expose the full customer hierarchy data as required for our use case.

My questions:

  1. Is there an existing standard API or communication scenario in S/4HANA Public Cloud that supports retrieving customer hierarchies?

  2. If not available as a standard API, is there a released CDS View or extension point that can be used via a custom communication scenario?

  3. Are there recommended approaches or best practices for handling this requirement in a public cloud environment, given the extensibility restrictions?

Any guidance or references would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance!

Maybe you can introduce me.
The scope of my question on a wider scale is to understand if a message can be tracked end to end from SRT_MONI to CPI to the next hop.
I see capability in the data but the timestamps I have do not match. Hence my question.

entry.title: Re: Storage bin BGM does not exist (check your entry)Message No. L9009
entry.content:
Does the storage bin exist in the LAGP 'Storage bins' table?

  • No - correct your entry or create the bin.
  • Yes - there may be a problem, provide more information (context).

entry.title: Universal parallel accounting activation in S/4HANA Cloud, Public edition
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Can green field customer for S/4HANA Cloud, Public edition select whether they activate Universal parallel accounting or not? Will it be installed as default and cannot deactivate if they use multiple ledgers and accounting principles with additional scope item 1GA or 2VA?

Kind Regards,

Eitaro

entry.title: 回复： 批量打印生产订单
entry.content:
您好，

目前没有对生产订单进行批量打印的程序。

由于生产订单的打印通常是由订单的release触发的，所以我们可以用批量release订单的方式来批量打印生产订单。 可以实现此功能的应用包括Manage Production Orders 和 Mass Processing Production Orders (COHV)。当然，这一方法只适用于还没有release的生产订单。对于已经release的，您就需要手动一个一个操作了。

在这个过程中可能会遇到打印顺序的问题，请参考下面的KBA：3493126 - When releasing production orders in mass which triggers the printing, the sequence of the output items is not the same as the sequence of the production orders 

更多关于生产订单打印的问题，请参考：3550037 - Typical issues and questions regarding production order output in S/4HANA Cloud public edition. 

Best Regards，
SAP Support
Matt

entry.title: Re: What are the free certification available for Consult...
entry.content:
Hello @Vijay_Handke,

SAP certifications are not free. You can take a free Learning Journey, but certification attempts are always paid (unless you have an SAP Learning Hub subscription). 

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Granting End User Access to a Specific Analytical Mod...
entry.content:
Hi,

As far as I know there is currently no standard way to achieve this.

We implemented a workaround for this using Data Access Controls.

Each of our models have a calculated field with a hardcoded value representing the ID of the model. Then we secure users access based on that hardcoded value. 

Models are still visible in SAC but users won't see data.

entry.title: Re: #COE#SW01 Change log for custom field
entry.content:
Look in the online help for Extensibility Inventory (in S/4 HANA Public Cmoud) :

e.g. Extensibility Inventory for CHANGE HISTORY.

  • Extensibility Inventory App
    • Search by Custom Field
      • Click on Change History

entry.title: Re: Preview/Print of a Document (Crystal Report) not working
entry.content:
Hi,

is your report based on DB tables or a command? If tables, check that you don't use any UDO/UDT which is not present in the "new database, and if using Command make sure you do not use hardcoded schema.

BR,

Matija

entry.title: Re: PS: Partner Function
entry.content: Thank you for the blog. It is helpful.

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA "Profit Center" in line item 0o1 not filled d...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP community blog.

There can be lot of reasons for this error. Anyways check the following first.

1) Check whether the PC field is made optional in the field status group for the corresponding GL.

2) Check whether the Corresponding document type is assign for document splitting.

3) Check whether the Corresponding GL is a P&L a/c if so whether is created as cost element. If so whether default cost center/ Profit center is assigned in OKB9.

I’m more assuming it’s coming from document splitting issue as the error message family starts by GLT*

Please come back to me when you have checkeds the points up above.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: How to call BuildProcessAutomation's TriggerAPI from RAP
entry.content: If implemented in RAP format, no runtime errors will occur, but the BPA workflow will not be started. The error when implemented in ABAP format is as follows

entry.content: I think this gives wrong answer since there is no indexing in hashed tables

best regards

Renaud

entry.content: Did you check App Id F3041 Import Supplier Invoices (for Accounts Payable Accountant - Procurement) if available in your version?

entry.title: FieldGlass integration for requisition creation
entry.content:
For a customer we are trying to connect SAP Fieldglass with SAP SuccessFactors. We are running into the issue that on the one hand SAP Fieldglass needs to use SAP Identity Access Services (IAS) as the identity provider to be able to login (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/configuration-sap-fieldglass-sso-with-sap-cloud-identity-services-identity/ba-p/13747208) and on the other hand SAP SuccessFactors needs to be setup as the identity provider to be able to create requisitions from positions (https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-employee-central/using-sap-fieldglass-with-employee-central/using-saml-single-sign-on-sso).

We are not really getting anywhere with SAP support as we are running in circles. In SuccessFactors Provisioning it shows that the integration with Fieldglass is not supported.

SAP Fieldglass only support the configuration of a single identity provider.

This means we are left with the choice to either be able to login to Fieldglass or be able to create requisitions.

Has anyone gotten the integration between SuccessFactors and Fieldglass to work properly?

Some additional documentation:

  • https://me.sap.com/notes/3468737/E
  • https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIELDGLASS_INTEGRATION/31c37f12fb734bd5a3b6039108a9c3ad/43347255fcb745aaa4f52764441fa50b.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Finding Data Source for Fiori Application Value Help...
entry.content:
Hi Shankar,
You can try to open the application`s CDS View in ADT and find such annotation:

@Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{ entity:
{ name: '{Name of a value help`s source}', element: 'Name of a value help`s fied '}}]

So, in a such case you`ll have to either change the source entity or create a new one and place it in the annotation.

BR,
Vadym

entry.title: Re: Granting End User Access to a Specific Analytical Mod...
entry.content:
Thank you for the response. Our requirement is that users should not see any unrelated Analytical Views in their access.

In my opinion, compared to BW, Datasphere is quite immature.. I hope to see improvements in this area soon.

entry.title: Archiving suggested for JEST Table- need support
entry.content:
Hello Team,

SAP recommended to plan Archiving for JEST table. 

With reference to recommendation on archiving JEST table, we checked and observed that there are many objects present in JEST table which is connected with different modules (like MM, FICO, etc.)
Please suggest: Which objects in JEST table should we consider?

 

Regards,

BASIS Team.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Archiving suggested for JEST Table- need support
entry.content:
Dear Basis Team,

The table JEST (as well as JCDS JSTO,...) belongs to the status management area. The mentioned tables contain information of objects and their assigned status - like for example for production orders, WBS, elements,...
Unfortunately tables JCDS, JEST, JSTO,... cannot be archived directly, there is no archiving object for it. That is because, as mentioned, the status information in tables JSTO and JEST is only a logical appendix of various master data objects like internal orders, production orders, WBS elements and many others. To reduce the data on table JEST etc., it is necessary to find out what leading objects they belong to and then archive them using the respective leading archiving object.

To get an overview of how data in JEST (and hence JSTO and JCDS as subsequent tables) is distributed across the objects I would recommend to use transaction TAANA with the table analysis for table JSTO because only table JSTO has a field OBTYP which describes what object the record refers to (object type can be decoded using transaction BS13 or table TJ03). So an analysis for table JSTO , field OBTYP would be a good start to see where data archiving could help to reduce the size of status related data base tables like JCDS.

By archiving these leading objects, the status management tables as JCDS should get archived too.

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA "Profit Center" in line item 0o1 not filled d...
entry.content: There are some KBAs  on this error message, such as 2921282 - GLT2201 - Balancing field Profit Center in line item not filled which contains a Customizing check list.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP managed scenario dumping CX_CSP_ACT_INTERNAL
entry.content: Hi @akil-k. Thank you for you help. I deleted the records using app. Then I checked the drafts table to see if they were gone and they are gone.

entry.title: FAQ – Migrating from SAP Business Network for Logistics NEO to Multi-Cloud
entry.content:
As SAP will phase out the Neo Integration Platform on May 16, 2025, customers of SAP Business Network for Logistics (BN4L) must transition to the Multi-Cloud platform to ensure business continuity. This document consolidates frequently asked questions from real-world projects and the official SAP migration guides.

Q: What happens to Freight Order (FO) or Freight Settlement Document (FSD) created on Neo if they remain open after May 16, 2025?

A: Freight Orders and Freight Settlement Documents that were originally created and sent via the Neo integration platform will continue to work after May 16, 2025, as long as the Neo connection is not deleted.

  • Follow-up messages (e.g. confirmation, invoice) for those documents will continue to be routed via Neo.
  • SAP will maintain backward compatibility to ensure that messages related to Neo-originated documents are processed successfully.
  • ⚠️Important: Do not delete or modify your Neo connections in the System Connections app. These connections are still required for processing existing documents even after the official Neo deprecation date.

Q: After the Shipper migrates from Neo to Multi-Cloud, how are Carrier responses (Order Confirmation, Invoice) routed for Freight Orders created before and after the migration?

A: The document routing logic depends entirely on where the FO (Freight Order) was originally sent from. Even if the Shipper and/or Carrier migrate, the platform used to send the original FO determines the message return path.

Below are five common scenarios that illustrate how documents are routed:

Scenario 1:

The Shipper sends a Freight Order (FO) from Neo, then migrates to Multi-Cloud, while the Carrier remains on Neo

 Result:

  • Carrier follow-up responses (e.g., confirmation, invoice) for that FO will still go through Neo, even after the Shipper has migrated.
  • New FOs sent from Multi-Cloud after migration will follow the Multi-Cloud path.

            On day 1, the shipper sends out a Freight document, FO1, from NEO to the carrier, the carrier hasn’t responded/confirmed the FO1

            On day 2, the shipper migrates to Multi-Cloud, then all new documents, like FO2 and FQ3, will be sent via Multi-Cloud

            On day 3, the carrier confirms the FO1 from the carrier portal after the shipper migrates to Multi-Cloud, then the FO1 document will continue using the NEO CPI platform and routing to shipper S4 even after May 16th

            On day 4, the carrier confirms FO2, then it will go through MultiCloud

            The response of FO1 is routed as follows: Carrier → (via Neo) → Network  → (via Neo) → Shipper

            The response of FO2 is routed as follows: Carrier → (via Neo) → Network → (via Multi-Cloud) →Shipper

Scenario 2:

The Shipper on Multi-Cloud, sends a FO to a Carrier who is still on Neo

Result:

  • If the Carrier is a web carrier, the message flow is automatically handled by BN4L and works without any issue.
  • If the Carrier is EDI/API integrated:
    • The FO is routed as follows: Shipper → (via Multi-Cloud) → Network →  (via Neo) → Carrier
    • The follow-up response is routed as follows: Carrier → (via Neo) → Network → (via Multi-Cloud) →Shipper

Scenario 3:

The Shipper sends a FO from Neo, then migrates, while the Carrier is already on Multi-Cloud

Result:

  • Even if the Carrier is already on Multi-Cloud, the Carrier’s follow-up response to that FO will still be routed via Neo, because the FO was originally sent from Neo.
  • The follow-up response is routed as follows: Carrier → (via Multi-Cloud) → Network → (via Neo) →Shipper

Scenario 4:

The Shipper sends a FO from Neo, and both the Shipper and Carrier migrate to Multi-Cloud later

Result:

  • For the FO originally created on Neo, Neo will still be used for any follow-up documents (confirmation/invoice).
  • The follow-up response is routed as follows: Carrier → (via Multi-Cloud) → Network → (via Neo) →Shipper

Scenario 5:

The Shipper on Multi-Cloud sends a FO to the Carrier on Neo, and the Carrier migrates afterward

 Result:

  • Multi-Could will be used for any follow-up documents
  • The FO initially flows: Shipper →(via Multi-Cloud) → Network → (via Neo) → Carrier
  • Follow-up Response flows: Carrier → (via Multi-Cloud) → Network → (via Multi-Cloud) →Shipper
  • Once the Carrier migrates, new documents (including responses to new FOs) will flow entirely via Multi-Cloud.

Q: When we say “the flow triggered by Neo will come back via Neo,” which documents are included in this flow? Does it include only the FO and order confirmation, or does it also include the invoice?

A: Yes, the term “flow” refers to the entire lifecycle of a freight order document — not just the FO and confirmation, but also invoice documents, if they refer to that FO.

📌Conclusion: Any follow-up documents (e.g., confirmation, invoice) is part of the original FO flow and follows the same integration platform used when the FO was created. That’s why it’s important not to delete Neo connections if open FO documents still exist on that platform

Q: Can Shippers and Carriers operate on different CPI platforms (Neo vs Multi-Cloud)?

A: Yes, SAP supports a hybrid scenario during the migration period. Shippers can remain on Neo while the Carriers already migrate to Multi-Cloud, or vice versa. BN4L correctly routes messages between the two platforms.

Q: For the migration from Neo to Multi-Cloud, do I need to create a new web service in SOAManager to support the new CPI connection?

A: No, you don’t need to create a new web service.
You can continue using the existing SOAManager service in your S/4HANA system. What you need to do is update the endpoint URL to the new Multi-Cloud platform endpoint.

For example, the new endpoint should point to:

 https://api.logistics.eu.business-network.cloud.sap/cxf/lbn/b2b/soap/v1

(or the corresponding us.business-network.cloud.sap URL based on your region).

Follow-up: If I change the endpoint in SOAManager to the Multi-Cloud CPI URL, will this affect old documents that were originally processed via Neo?

A: No, it doesn’t affect them — as long as you do not delete the old Neo connection in the System Connections app in BN4L.

Here’s why:

  • Old documents (e.g., FO/FSD) created before the migration are still tied to the Neo platform connection in BN4L.
  • Even after you update the endpoint in SOAManager, the BN4L system keeps using the existing Neo connection to send back follow-up responses (such as confirmation or invoice) for those old documents.
  • Only new documents created after the endpoint switch will flow through Multi-Cloud end-to-end.

Key takeaway:

The SOAManager update affects only new outbound messages from your backend system.
Old document flows will continue to function correctly as long as the Neo connection entry in BN4L remains intact.

Q: We noticed that some message types like TransportationEventBulkNotification, TransportationOrderChargeElementRequest, and InvoiceRequest are still being processed via the Neo platform. Is this expected?

A: Yes, this behavior is expected and correct.

These messages are follow-up responses sent by the Carrier in response to a FO originally sent by the Shipper. BN4L routes follow-up responses based on the platform used to send the original FO.

For example:

If a Freight Order was originally sent from your backend via the Neo platform,

all related follow-up messages (like confirmations, events, charges, and even invoices that reference that FO) will also be sent via Neo, regardless of whether you’ve migrated to Multi-Cloud.

Important:
This is why it's critical not to delete or deactivate the old Neo connection in the System Connections app. BN4L uses that connection for all documents originally processed via Neo.

Q:My existing Neo connection certificates are expiring, and I want to update them to ensure business continuity. What should I do?

A: Ensuring connectivity should be the highest priority. It’s possible to add new certificates to your existing Neo connections. You can do the same ensuring business continuity and then plan your migration to the new Multi-Cloud platform accordingly.

Q:I have completed the migration and see that new integration logs in the Manage Integration Logs app point to the Multi-Cloud platform. However, B2B messages to my carrier (with flow direction ‘Network to External System’ and ‘External System to Network’) are executing via the old ‘Neo’ integration platform. Is there anything pending from my side?  

A: There are some configuration changes that freemium customers such as carriers must do at their end to migrate from Neo to Multi-Cloud platform. This is an independent activity that each customer in the network must do. Until all your related carriers have done the same, you’ll find some B2B integration logs that are executed via the old integration platform. There are no technical actions pending from your side in this case. If you are in contact with your partners/carriers, you can inform them about this pending action at their end. Please find a migration guide targeted for our freemium customers here.

Final tips

  • For Shippers, DO NOT delete Neo connections
  • Perform full regression testing before go-live
  • Monitor integration logs for flow paths and failures

entry.title: Re: Performance Objectives - Story reporting
entry.content:
Hi Mirjam,

There is no other way to do that, then to give them access to see goals + access to PMGM Story reporting. However, you can try to do it carefully, for example, maybe test if you can provide the access to goals of employees, but not allowing to access it on PMGM side somehow. That would be enough for Stories. Then, in your report just show the necessary measures and not more (don't add Goal names and descriptions in the Query) and share it with them with an option "View only". Normally, if they will not see anything unless they already have have broad access to build their own reports (which also means they can edit reports). In that case they see more, but you can always check their RBP to be sure. 

Kind regards,

Olga

entry.title: Re: Universal parallel accounting activation in S/4HANA C...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP community blog.

For greenfield implementations of SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) is automatically active and can't be switched off. It's part of the core architecture now, so customers don’t get to choose whether or not to use it - it comes enabled by default.

This applies even if you plan to use additional scope items like 1GA (Alternative Accounting Principles) or 2VA (Parallel Valuation). Those scope items build on top of UPA; they don't replace it or offer a way around it.

In short: if you're starting fresh on S/4HANA Public Cloud, you're working with Universal Parallel Accounting whether you're using multiple ledgers or not.

 

I hope this will answer your main question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: What is 91-Club Invite-Code? 1627417228387
entry.content: 91-Club Invite-Code is "1627417228387 ".

entry.title: Re: Need API to Fetch Customer Hierarchies in S/4HANA Pub...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP community blog.

 

To retrieve customer hierarchy data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, there’s no out-of-the-box API that exposes the full hierarchy structure directly. The standard Business Partner OData API (API_BUSINESS_PARTNER) lets you pull core customer master data, but it doesn’t include hierarchy relationships like parent-child customer assignments used for pricing or reporting.

 

To access hierarchy data, create a custom CDS view using standard interface views such as I_CustomerHierarchyNode and I_CustomerHierarchy. From there, you can expose it as a custom OData service using a communication scenario.

 

This gives you a flexible way to pull the relevant hierarchy structure  for example, customer, hierarchy ID, higher-level customer, etc. and integrate it with other systems or reports.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: SAP分析云指南针：分布演练指南（二）
entry.content:
这是关于使用指南针进行模拟的两篇博客系列的第二部分。该博客从触发方案运行的步骤之后开始介绍。作为回顾，以下是两篇博客的结构： 

博客（一）： 

#1 -启动模拟 

#2 -配置动因 

#3 -运行模拟 

博客（二）： 

#1 -结果解读 

#2 -保存模拟与方案对比 

#3 -协作与分享 

让我们继续深入吧！

#4 –解读模拟结果

结果的组成部分

 

一旦计算完成，模拟的概率分布将以图表的形式显示，该图表分为三个部分：乐观情况、悲观情况和实际情况。默认情况下，它们的定义如下：悲观情况（结果中最低的5%）、乐观情况（结果中最高的5%）和实际情况（中间的90%结果）。可以通过“案例设置”配置更改百分比的定义，但5/90/5的百分比划分是统计学中最常用的情况。每种情况对应的范围在图表上可见，并且在下方为三种情况预留的三个图中也可以看到。您还会看到一个滑块，它停在当前基线处，即模型中目标KPI当前记录的值，该值也在X轴上用“基线（当前值）”标记。

理解结果 

解读模拟结果需要综合考虑三个主要组成部分：（1）图表（2）当前基线的位置以及显示的百分比（3）下面方框中所示的结果范围。 

在上面的例子中，你可以看到当前基线位于图表的实际情况区域。这表明模型中当前记录的值在获得的结果范围内，但不在边缘情况（最高或最低的5%值）内，因此从统计学角度来看，它有实现的现实可能性。滑块右侧的13%表示有13%的机会实现高于当前基线2.3504亿美元的值。但请注意，由于乐观情况（在底部最右边的框中）仅延伸至3.40133亿美元，这意味着有13%的机会实现2.5438亿至3.0133亿美元之间的值。同样地，对于滑块左侧的87%，它表示有87%的机会实现1.4422亿至0.8167亿美元之间的值，而0.8167亿美元是悲观情况下可实现的最低值。 

现在，大家可能会好奇为什么图表中有一个滑块。这个滑块实际上允许探索与特定值相关的概率。如果你想知道在场景设定的条件下，达到2亿美元的概率有多大，就直接把滑块拉到2亿美元的位置；或者在输入框里填入“200”即可。这样，左右两侧显示的百分比将重新计算，并应按照上一段中解释的方式进行解释。

#5 – 保存模拟结果和方案对比 

保存模拟结果的重要性 

完成模拟后，可以通过点击模拟或方案名称旁边的保存图标，将其保存到文件资源库中。保存模拟有两个好处：（1）可以在稍后的时间点返回并将其结果与实际表现进行比较，从而洞察所做的假设的准确性以及观察到的结果。如果长期进行这种比较，这种方法将有助于理解重要的驱动因素以及如何更准确地进行模拟。（2）保存模拟允许创建多个方案*，可以将它们相互比较，以了解通过不同假设可以获得的结果。 

（*在切换到另一个方案工作之前，您需要先保存每个方案中的更改。这是为了减少在比较方案时对结果实际性的混淆。） 

方案对比 

在上面的例子中，你可以看到可以通过标题栏中的下拉菜单选择另一个方案。所选方案的结果将出现在底部，分别显示在为悲观情况、实际情况和乐观情况的结果预留的三个框中。这样可以让你一目了然地比较不同方案的结果，立即了解每个方案在每种情况下表现如何。 

温馨提示 

建议首先模拟一个方案，其数值范围反映现实生活中存在的不确定性，以便了解波动对业务的冲击程度，以及公司在最坏和最好情况下所处的位置。在获得这些洞察后，再创建更多方案，其数值范围反映公司通过应对策略减轻风险的假设。随后可以对这些方案进行比较，以协助战略决策。注意，在比较方案时，一个在最坏情况下结果较差的方案，可能在乐观情况下会带来更好的结果，因此在评估影响时，始终建议查看结果的两端。 

还要记得为每个方案给出清晰的描述，说明所给数值范围背后的意图。因为所有方案可能使用相同的驱动因素，所以过了一段时间后，仅通过观察数字很难回忆起每个方案数值输入背后的策略意图。如果可能的话，先从简短的描述开始，比如“在亚洲进行投资营销”，然后逐步完善。描述将是你的得力助手；它实际上与图表和数值范围一样，是提供建模策略线索的最有用功能之一。 

还有以下几点也值得注意：根据您使用指南针的目的不同，您想要的答案有时可能包含在结果图表中，有时则包含在动因列表中的“值配置”里。 

比如：在第一个数值范围反映现实生活中不确定性的方案中，你想要的答案是可能出现的风险会产生多大影响的分布，这个答案在结果图表里能找到。但是，当通过进一步的方案来对应对策略进行建模时，每个方案的结果图表更多地成了你假设可能有效的参考。所以，你真正想要的答案其实是在那个结果最让你满意的特定方案的“值配置”里，它能让你明白，为了达到可接受的风险情境，应该给选定的动因设定什么样的范围。

#6 – 协作与共享 

跨部门收集假设 

完成模拟后，你可能希望与同事或决策者分享你的结果。或者，你可能希望邀请负责的同事与你一起进行模拟。使用指南针，你可以与多位同事一起进行模拟，甚至可以跨部门进行。协作功能至关重要，因为很多时候，不同地区和产品的动因不确定性（即每个动因的最小值和最大值）的信息并不是集中存放的，而是分散在不同的（全球）部门中。这里的协作功能将为集体企业模拟提供一个结构化的环境。 

私有和公用方案 

你可能已经注意到，当你开始模拟时，创建的方案始终是私有方案，这意味着它仅对你可见，其他人无法看到。这确保了模拟过程中的保密性。一旦你决定要向同事展示某个方案，你可以通过发布方案并共享保存的模拟结果来实现，就像你与他们共享故事或模型一样。请注意，发布方案是在指南针界面中完成的（在方案标题栏中），而共享则是在文件资源库中进行的。如果你的发布图标处于禁用状态，这意味着你的方案中存在未保存的更改。只需点击保存图标，然后之后再发布即可。 

与你共享模拟的同事将只能看到处于只读状态的公用方案。如果需要，他们可以创建公用方案的私有副本，添加自己的值配置，运行方案，然后再次发布，可以选择覆盖当前的公用方案，或者作为新的方案发布。请注意，虽然可以通过移动滑块等方式探索公用方案的结果，但诸如此类的更改不会被保存。 

以上就是目前的全部内容了，希望这个逐步指导能让你更深入地了解如何利用指南针，不仅帮助你理解当前面临的风险，还能了解如何减轻这些风险。 

我们一直在努力改进指南针！请在influence portal中给我们留言，告诉我们你认为下一步可以增强哪些功能。我们会密切关注该门户，并倾听你的声音！ 

原文链接：A Step-By-Step Walkthrough for SAP Analytics Cloud... - SAP Community

原作者：ChenNee

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to generate alert for BTP Audit Log using Alert Notification Service?
entry.content: i am facing issue. although i got test email but when role is assigned i am not getting email notification
